all.hypothesis: Calibrating tryptophan's degradation rate (the one reactive amino acid in venus_005) against acid concentration provides a chemical clock that, if a sample could be returned, would constrain venus_001's modeled cloud acidity to ±5% w/w.

proposed_experiment.setup: Build a tryptophan degradation-rate vs. acidity calibration via venus_005's NMR method; propose it as a sample-return acidity assay for venus_001's models.
proposed_experiment.metric: Acidity inferred from tryptophan degradation vs. model-predicted acidity.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_001's acidity from binary-condensation modeling alone.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Requires sample return; in-situ NMR is infeasible.

all.impact: Offers an independent chemical check on a key cloud parameter that is currently model-bound.

all.research_question: Can venus_003's vibrational-fingerprint library give venus_001 molecule-specific IR diagnostics to disentangle mixed cloud composition in mission data?
all.hypothesis: DFT IR fingerprints from venus_003 will provide at least three non-overlapping diagnostic bands enabling venus_001 to discriminate sulfuric acid, SO3 polymers, and a candidate organic in a single mid-IR spectrum.

proposed_experiment.setup: Assemble venus_003 DFT IR spectra for the relevant species; test separability in a synthetic mixed-aerosol spectrum at mission resolution.
proposed_experiment.metric: Number of resolvable diagnostic bands at realistic spectral resolution.
proposed_experiment.baseline: Bulk refractive-index fitting without molecular assignments.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Band overlap and broadening in concentrated-acid condensed phase.

all.impact: Improves compositional inversion of Venus cloud spectra.

all.research_question: Can venus_005's reproducibility and data-availability practices (Zenodo deposition, documented spectrometer/processing pipeline) be transferred to make venus_003's computational results independently verifiable?
all.hypothesis: Adopting venus_005's open-data discipline — depositing input geometries, full method parameters, and output energies — will allow independent recomputation that reproduces venus_003's key ΔG values within numerical tolerance, exposing any undocumented assumptions.

proposed_experiment.setup: Package venus_003's calculations (geometries, SMD parameters, basis, software versions) in a Zenodo-style archive; have an independent party recompute headline results.
proposed_experiment.metric: Reproduction error on key ΔG values; number of previously undocumented parameters surfaced.
proposed_experiment.baseline: The originally reported venus_003 values.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Implicit-solvent and software-version dependencies can cause non-trivial irreproducibility even with full disclosure.

all.impact: Brings experimental-grade reproducibility norms to a computational study, a prerequisite for it to be trusted in habitability debates.

all.research_question: Can a venus_003-style computational benchmark validate that B3LYP-D3BJ/SMD is accurate enough for sulfuric-acid organic chemistry before it is trusted for Venus habitability predictions?
all.hypothesis: Benchmarking B3LYP-D3BJ/6-31+G(d,p)/SMD against higher-level methods (DLPNO-CCSD(T), explicit-solvent QM/MM) on a small amino-acid + H2SO4 test set will reveal errors small enough (<10 kJ/mol on relative barriers) to justify the cheaper protocol for full-scale screening.

proposed_experiment.setup: Select 3-4 representative amino-acid degradation reactions; compute relative barriers at B3LYP-D3BJ/SMD and at reference levels; quantify deviations.
proposed_experiment.metric: Mean and max absolute error in relative barriers versus the reference method.
proposed_experiment.baseline: High-level coupled-cluster / explicit-solvent reference energies.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Reference calculations in concentrated acid require large explicit-solvent clusters, making the benchmark itself computationally expensive.

all.impact: Establishes the methodological trust needed before any DFT-based Venus organic-stability claim can be taken as predictive rather than suggestive.

all.research_question: Can venus_005's empirical D2SO4-in-D2O solvent system (98% and 81% w/w) replace venus_003's water-parametrized SMD with experimentally grounded solvent properties for the Venus acid medium?
all.hypothesis: Replacing venus_003's ad-hoc dielectric-modified water SMD with parameters fit to measured 98%/81% sulfuric-acid solvent properties will materially change computed heteropolymer stabilities, indicating that the original solvent treatment is a significant source of uncertainty.

proposed_experiment.setup: Derive SMD/implicit-solvent parameters from measured H2SO4-H2O physical properties at 98% and 81%; recompute venus_003's key heteropolymer reactions; compare to the original water-SMD results.
proposed_experiment.metric: Shift in computed ΔG for heteropolymer formation/degradation between old and new solvent models.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_003's original modified-water SMD results.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Reliable implicit-solvent parametrization needs experimental solvation/dielectric data for concentrated H2SO4 that may be incomplete in the literature.

all.impact: Addresses the most criticized assumption in venus_003 — using a water solvent model for sulfuric acid — with empirically grounded inputs.

all.research_question: Can the systematic 'simple-to-complex' experimental methodology of venus_005 (test monomers first, then derivatives) be transferred to structure venus_003's computational program around experimentally tractable building blocks?
all.hypothesis: Re-organizing venus_003's heteropolymer study to first compute and then experimentally test stability of isolated monomers and dimers (mirroring venus_005's pilot-then-expand design) will reveal that monomer stability does not predict polymer stability, exposing a gap the all-computational approach missed.

proposed_experiment.setup: Define a monomer→dimer→oligomer ladder for the S/P heteropolymers; compute stability at each rung; pair with the venus_005 NMR assay on the synthesizable rungs.
proposed_experiment.metric: Stability as a function of chain length, computed and (where possible) measured.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_003's polymer-focused predictions without the monomer ladder.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Defining chemically realistic intermediate oligomers and obtaining them for the experimental rungs.

all.impact: Imports a proven experimental design philosophy that de-risks the heteropolymer hypothesis by grounding it in testable increments.

all.research_question: Can venus_005's measured concentration effect (98% vs 81% H2SO4 reactivity differences) test whether venus_003's predicted heteropolymer stabilities are robust across the acidity range actually spanned by Venus clouds?
all.hypothesis: Heteropolymer linkages predicted stable by venus_003 will show measurably different stability at 81% versus 98% acid, demonstrating that a single-acidity computation is insufficient and that water activity is a decisive variable.

proposed_experiment.setup: Compute and (for available analogs) measure heteropolymer-linkage stability at both 81% and 98% acid; quantify the acidity sensitivity.
proposed_experiment.metric: Difference in stability/hydrolysis rate between the two concentrations.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_003's single-condition computed stability.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Capturing water-activity / proton-activity effects, which implicit solvation handles poorly, in the computation.

all.impact: Tests whether venus_003's habitability conclusion survives the real range of Venus cloud acidity rather than one idealized point.

all.research_question: Can DFT/TD-DFT-derived SO2 and SO3 reaction energetics and UV-Vis cross-sections (Domain B) replace the parametrized chemistry that drives the photochemical H2SO4 production rate underlying the supersaturation term in the Dai 2022a and CARMA cloud models (Domain A)?
all.hypothesis: If TD-DFT excitation energies (lowest 50 singlet/triplet states) and computed barriers for the SO2-to-SO3-to-H2SO4 pathway are used to set the photochemical H2SO4 production rate that Dai 2022a maps to supersaturation, then the predicted cloud mass loading and acidity will differ from the empirically-parametrized baseline by a quantifiable amount, and will be more sensitive to the assumed SO3 condensed-phase equilibrium (monomer/gamma-trimer/alpha,beta-polymer) than the current models assume.

proposed_experiment.setup: Use the Domain B Gaussian 16/TD-DFT workflow to generate SO2 photoabsorption cross-sections and SO3 formation/polymerization energetics, convert to a height-resolved H2SO4 production rate, and substitute it into the Dai 2022a supersaturation expression and CARMA's chemistry source term.
proposed_experiment.metric: Resulting cloud mass loading, modal radius, and acidity profile, and their sensitivity to which SO3 phase (per v3_005) is assumed as feedstock.
proposed_experiment.baseline: Dai 2022a / CARMA runs using their existing literature photochemical production rates.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Gas-phase DFT barriers omit pressure/third-body and actinic-flux effects, so mapping molecular energetics to an altitude-resolved production rate requires a separate radiative-transfer/kinetics layer.

all.impact: Tightens the weakest link in Venus cloud models, the assumed H2SO4 source, by grounding it in first-principles SO2/SO3 chemistry.

all.research_question: Can venus_005's sample-preparation and dissolution protocol (mg-scale loading, 80 °C dissolution, sealed-vial storage) define realistic concentration and phase constraints that venus_003's gas/implicit-phase calculations ignore?
all.hypothesis: Imposing venus_005's experimentally realistic solubility and concentration limits on venus_003's reactions will show that some computationally favorable heteropolymer formations are infeasible because the monomers are not sufficiently soluble in concentrated acid.

proposed_experiment.setup: Estimate or measure solubility of venus_003's monomer candidates under venus_005-style conditions; flag computed reactions that exceed realistic concentrations.
proposed_experiment.metric: Fraction of predicted-favorable reactions that survive the solubility/concentration filter.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_003's predictions without solubility constraints.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Measuring or estimating solubility for exotic carbon-free monomers with no literature data.

all.impact: Injects a physical-reality filter (solubility/phase) that thermodynamic computation alone omits, pruning unrealistic candidates.

all.research_question: Can venus_005's sample-preparation and sealed-vial methodology define the handling requirements for a Venus cloud sample-return mission described in venus_001?
all.hypothesis: venus_005's protocol (sealed glass vials, controlled temperature, concentrated D2SO4 handling) maps onto a feasible containment spec showing that organics in a returned 90%+ acid sample remain analytically intact for >months at 18-24°C.

proposed_experiment.setup: Translate venus_005's lab storage/stability conditions into containment requirements for venus_001's sample-return concept; test stability over storage-relevant durations.
proposed_experiment.metric: Analyte integrity vs. storage time/temperature under flight-relevant containment.
proposed_experiment.baseline: No defined organic-preservation spec in venus_001's mission discussion.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Flight containment of hot, corrosive concentrated acid differs sharply from a benchtop sealed vial.

all.impact: Provides an evidence-based preservation requirement for a high-priority but chemically hostile sample-return target.

all.research_question: Can venus_005's documented use of reference spectral databases (SDBS) and acid-specific D2SO4 referencing supply the missing experimental NMR-shift calibration that venus_003 would need to predict its heteropolymers' spectra?
all.hypothesis: Adopting venus_005's acid-medium NMR referencing conventions will let venus_003-style GIAO calculations produce predicted shifts directly comparable to future experimental spectra of synthesized heteropolymers, with calibration error under typical DFT tolerance.

proposed_experiment.setup: Use venus_005's D2SO4 reference data to build an acid-medium NMR-shift calibration; apply it to venus_003-style computed shifts of heteropolymer model compounds.
proposed_experiment.metric: Calibration residuals on known compounds; predicted shift tables for heteropolymers.
proposed_experiment.baseline: Uncalibrated gas-phase / water-referenced computed shifts.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Few experimental reference compounds exist for carbon-free S/P species, limiting calibration coverage.

all.impact: Equips the computational study with an experimentally anchored prediction tool, enabling eventual lab confirmation.

all.research_question: Can venus_003's quantum-chemical thermochemistry replace the empirical assumptions in venus_001's H2SO4 formation/recycling chemistry with first-principles reaction free energies?
all.hypothesis: Substituting venus_003-style DFT Gibbs free energies for key H2SO4-cycle steps into venus_001's cloud chemistry will shift the predicted cloud-base H2SO4 production rate by >20% relative to the literature-rate baseline.

proposed_experiment.setup: Compute reaction free energies/barriers for SO3 + H2O and related steps with the venus_003 DFT+SMD protocol; feed them into venus_001's H2SO4-recycling budget.
proposed_experiment.metric: H2SO4 production rate and resulting cloud mass loading.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_001's tabulated/empirical reaction rates.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Connecting gas-phase DFT barriers to the temperature- and pressure-dependent kinetics needed by the atmospheric model.

all.impact: Reduces reliance on extrapolated lab rates in Venus cloud chemistry, tightening uncertainty on the sulfur cycle.

all.research_question: Can venus_001's cloud-base evaporation/recycling chemistry predict the fate of venus_005's stable amino acids when their host droplets evaporate in the hot lower atmosphere?
all.hypothesis: venus_001's cloud-base thermal profile will exceed the temperature at which venus_005's acid-stable amino acids thermally decompose, so chemical (acid) stability does not guarantee survival of the full cloud-to-surface cycle.

proposed_experiment.setup: Combine venus_001's cloud-base T (~350-450 K) and H2SO4-recycling region with venus_005's amino-acid set; flag thermal-decomposition risk.
proposed_experiment.metric: Cloud-base temperature vs. amino-acid thermal decomposition onset.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_005's 18-80°C lab temperature range only.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Thermal decomposition data for amino acids in concentrated acid above 80°C are missing.

all.impact: Identifies the thermal—not acid—bottleneck for organic survival in the Venus cloud life cycle.

all.research_question: Can venus_001's GCM-coupling-ready Dai 2022a model give venus_003's heteropolymer chemistry a transport context to predict whether photochemical production keeps pace with sedimentation losses?
all.hypothesis: Coupling venus_003's polymer formation rate into venus_001's transport/sedimentation framework will reveal that sedimentation removes polymers from the photochemically active 60-65 km layer faster than they form, capping steady-state abundance an order of magnitude below detection.

proposed_experiment.setup: Add venus_003's production term to venus_001's transport+sedimentation scheme; solve for steady-state polymer profile.
proposed_experiment.metric: Production vs. sedimentation-loss balance; steady-state abundance.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_003's production-only thermodynamic estimate.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Estimating polymer particle settling velocity from molecular-scale inputs.

all.impact: Tests whether dynamics, not chemistry, is the real limit on exotic-polymer abundance.

all.research_question: Can venus_003's Gibbs-free-energy thermochemistry quantify the feasibility of the SO3-polymer feedstock reactions venus_001 needs for non-sulfuric cloud components?
all.hypothesis: venus_003's free-energy calculations will show SO3 polymerization is exergonic at cloud-top temperatures, supporting venus_001's invocation of solid-sulfur species as a viable minor cloud component.

proposed_experiment.setup: Compute SO3 monomer/trimer/chain polymerization free energies with venus_003's protocol across the cloud-top temperature range.
proposed_experiment.metric: Polymerization Gibbs free energy vs. temperature.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_001's qualitative assertion of SO3-phase presence.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Condensed-phase polymer thermochemistry is hard to capture with molecular implicit-solvent DFT.

all.impact: Provides thermodynamic backing for a proposed non-sulfuric cloud constituent.

all.research_question: Can venus_003's bond-dissociation and photodegradation calculations supply venus_001 with chemical-lifetime values for its UV-absorber candidates under solar UV?
all.hypothesis: venus_003's computed photodissociation thresholds will show that several of venus_001's UV-absorber candidates photodegrade faster than their replenishment timescale, ruling them out on lifetime grounds.

proposed_experiment.setup: Use venus_003's DFT bond-dissociation/excited-state analysis to estimate photochemical lifetimes for venus_001's candidate absorbers under the Venus UV flux.
proposed_experiment.metric: Photochemical lifetime vs. required residence time for a persistent absorber.
proposed_experiment.baseline: venus_001's candidates assessed without lifetime constraints.
proposed_experiment.expected_bottleneck: Excited-state dissociation dynamics exceed the static TD-DFT picture in venus_003.

all.impact: Adds a photostability filter to the UV-absorber candidate field.
